institutionName: Orlando Health Orlando Regional Medical Center
verdictSummary: Zero PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE claims; merged ledger is internally consistent and safe to merge. The clerkship-programs page contains rich Tier 1 visiting-student content (VSLO, $50 fee, 4-week rotations, foreign-student CIE 701/702 pathway, M4 audience) but was correctly held at FUTURE_LANE_ONLY because source family is tagged GME_PAGE per P102 rule 5. System-level medical-professionals hub claims correctly held at HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED. No scope conflicts, no overclaims, no duplicates.
unfollowedSignals: clerkship-programs page is substantively a Tier 1 visiting-student clerkship spec (VSLO + paper app, M4 students, $50 fee, 4-week rotations, foreign-student CIE 701/702 pathway) but is tagged sourceFamily=GME_PAGE which blocks promotion to PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE; source-family reclassification is the correct unblocking lever (also flagged by ledger sc02)
Pediatric Residency 'M4 Away Rotations' link surfaced on three separate sources but the dedicated M4 Away Rotations page itself was not captured

deepRecoveryTasks.reason: clerkship-programs URL is tagged GME_PAGE which forces all Tier 1 clerkship claims to FUTURE_LANE_ONLY, yet the page literally describes a VSLO + paper-app visiting M4 clerkship with 4-week rotations, $50 fee, foreign-student CIE pathway, named coordinators, and 2026/2027 block dates. Ledger flags this internally (sc02).
deepRecoveryTasks.suggestedNarrowAction: Reclassify src_46 (orlandohealth.com/medical-professionals/graduate-medical-education/clerkship-programs) to a Tier 1 visiting-student source family in the classifier registry, then re-gate existing c01-c16 and a2_c01-a2_c09 claims for PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE eligibility, requiring campusApplicabilityProof for ORMC since policy is system-wide.
